settings.text:
We make one thing
for the cars you've kept

DCM·Works is a one-person studio making motorised display pads for 1:64 die-cast cars. One founder, one product line, and a position on the kind of objects adults should be allowed to keep on their desks.

It started with one car
and a 3D printer.

I bought a 7-Eleven liveried Hot Wheels at a convenience store in 2019. It sat on a shelf for four years.

One weekend in March 2024 I designed a small, motorised parking-lot diorama to give it somewhere to live. Printed it on an FDM in the garage, soldered the motor myself, put it on my desk. Two friends asked for one. Then four of theirs. Then strangers.

Twenty months later, DCM·Works is still a one-person studio working out of the same garage, shipping one product properly: the DriftPad. Same idea, better materials, the same car still on the prototype unit by the door.

settings.milestone_1_description: First diorama. 3D-printed. One motor. One car. One garage bench.
settings.milestone_2_description: 30 pads, hand-finished. Sold out in 11 days via DMs.
settings.milestone_3_description: Three benches, proper lighting, the CNC arrived.
settings.milestone_4_description: Brushless motor, integrated lighting, the version still shipped.
settings.milestone_5_description: Three editions. Proper packaging. The site you're reading.

settings.principle_1_description: I could ship a stand, a tray, a case, a bigger pad, a smaller one. I won't. One object, refined every year, is the entire road map. The catalogue is the point.
settings.principle_2_description: If you can hear the motor on a Zoom call, I shipped a bad unit. Mechanical silence is harder than a quieter motor — it's bearings, mounts, harmonics, and a week of QA per batch. I pay for it.
settings.principle_3_description: I don't talk about RPM or torque on the homepage. The DriftPad sells because of a feeling — the car you kept since you were nine, finally with somewhere to live. That feeling drives every decision after.
settings.principle_4_description: Twelve-month warranty is the floor, not the ceiling. Replaceable motors, replaceable mounts, replaceable lighting. I send a service kit; I don't send a new product.

settings.text: THREE BENCHES, ONE CNC, TWO FDM PRINTERS, AND THE ESPRESSO MACHINE THAT BUILT THE COMPANY. DOOR OPEN MOST MORNINGS.

settings.step_1_description: Each pad spends 38 minutes here. Motor seated, mounts indexed, lighting harness keyed.
settings.step_2_description: Every unit runs for 4 hours on a foam-isolated bench before it's boxed. I listen.
settings.step_3_description: Recycled molded pulp, no plastic. The car still goes on top for the photo.

settings.text:
DCM·Works is a one-person studio. The founder designs, prototypes, assembles, QAs, packs and answers your email. The benches are in the garage; the email comes back inside a day.

That's deliberate. As long as one pair of hands can finish every unit, every unit gets finished the same way.

"I sell one thing on purpose. The catalogue is the answer to a question — what is the most respectful piece of furniture you can build for a piece of plastic you bought when you were nine? — and the answer is one object, made well, and nothing else next to it."
